text.title: Release QA Cycle Template Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the release-qa-cycle-template methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale; the final rule is the skip-this-methodology gate referenced by the decision tree.

rule.statement: Cycle has exactly 6 ordered stages: strategy → smoke → bug-bash → perf → rollback-triggers → go/no-go. Skipping a stage requires an explicit waiver entry.
rule.rationale: Ordering matters: strategy frames the smoke, smoke is cheap pre-bash, bash informs perf, perf informs triggers, triggers feed go/no-go.

rule.statement: Each stage has a single named owner who signs off.
rule.rationale: Stage hand-off without ownership creates blame voids.

rule.statement: Each stage produces a binary exit (pass / fail / blocked). Soft outcomes promote partial-state releases.
Skipping or failing a stage requires a waiver record (approver + reason). Implicit skips are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Implicit skips show up at postmortem; explicit waivers are reviewable.

rule.statement: Cycle outputs a go/no-go record: stage outcomes, waivers, named approver, timestamp.
rule.rationale: Without a record, releases cannot be audited.

rule.statement: Every cycle ends with a 15-min retro recording 1 thing to keep + 1 thing to change in the next cycle.
rule.rationale: Continuous improvement only works with explicit retros.

rule.statement: If team is solo OR continuous-deploy with sub-1% blast radius OR pre-product, skip this methodology.
rule.rationale: Cycle overhead is wasted in those configurations.
